chunks.title: What INN-SIGHT is
chunks.text: INN-SIGHT helps hospitality designers stress-test hotel, homestay, and B&B options on a real map pin before building. You pick a site, assemble Option A vs Option B (structure + HVAC), run year stress, and get an investor-style memo with sourced numbers. Core kWh, cost, and CO2 come from a deterministic model (benchmarks + sim), not from inventing values in chat.

chunks.title: How year stress works
chunks.text: Run year stress simulates five extreme 48-hour weekends (heat-wave, summer shoulder, typical July, typical winter, deep cold)—not a full 8760h year. Outdoor temps prefer Open-Meteo ERA5 historical data for the selected lat/lng; if that fails, curated Toronto curves are used and labelled as fallback. Peak kW and STABLE/ELEVATED/CRITICAL strain come from the sim engine for Option A and B.

chunks.title: Option A vs Option B
chunks.text: Option A is typically concrete + central gas HVAC. Option B is typically mass timber + heat pumps. The memo recommends the greener option when implied carbon abatement cost is at or below Canada's federal ~$170/tCO2e benchmark; otherwise it may keep the cheaper path. Always cite the memo's comparison and recommendation fields when answering—do not invent new numbers.

chunks.title: Parcel acres and land use
chunks.text: Acres and land-use kind come from the selected OpenStreetMap empty-parcel polygon (approx. footprint). This is not a legal survey or vacant-lot registry. Approx pads may show acres without a tagged use.

chunks.title: Live site climate
chunks.text: Site climate (temp, humidity, wind, elevation) is live Open-Meteo forecast for the active pin. It is separate from the historical ERA5 curves used inside the stress engine.

chunks.title: Investor memo
chunks.text: The investor memo packs deterministic sim results: A/B costs, carbon, peak strain, recommendation, reasoning chain, narrative, and footnotes to benchmark sources. Gemini may write narrative prose over those numbers; without a key, a deterministic fallback narrative is used. Chat must only explain numbers present in the provided memo context.

chunks.title: Honesty ledger
chunks.text: INN-SIGHT labels estimates vs sourced benchmarks. Friction scores are heuristics, not surveys. Green parcels are OSM open land, not a legal registry. Annual energy uses CBECS-style intensities; stress windows are 48h extremes, not metered bills for the exact building.

chunks.title: Sign-in
chunks.text: When Auth0 is enabled, Google sign-in creates/syncs a profile. Past runs can be stored per user. Chat cannot change auth settings.
